overview.name: Shrivarshaa Sakhamuri
overview.title: Software Developer
description: I build real-world systems that handle scale, not just projects that stay on GitHub. Recently, I worked at Citi, where I engineered backend services and a real-time transaction dashboard used by fraud analysts.

The system processed 20K+ daily transactions, and I improved performance by up to 40% by optimizing APIs, redesigning database queries, and introducing Redis caching. I also contributed to a scalable architecture that supported 100K+ concurrent users with high availability and low latency.

My work sits at the intersection of backend engineering, cloud infrastructure, and performance optimization. I’ve built microservices using Python (FastAPI) and Java (Spring Boot), designed hybrid data systems (MySQL + NoSQL), and deployed production-ready solutions on AWS using CloudFormation and CI/CD pipelines.

Beyond that, I’ve worked on projects like building an end-to-end CI/CD pipeline on AWS and developing machine learning models for real-time analytics. I enjoy breaking down complex systems, identifying bottlenecks, and designing solutions that are both efficient and scalable.

I recently completed my Master’s in Computer Science at the University of Texas at Arlington (4.0 GPA), where I specialized in Artificial Intelligence and Cloud Computing.Right now, I’m focused on growing as a backend/full-stack engineer,building systems that are reliable, performant, and designed with real-world constraints in mind.
